title: The Verifiable Tournament
tagline: A live leaderboard nobody can rig: every rank is ECDSA-signed and freshness-stamped.
holy_shit: You can prove the leaderboard wasn't faked: each entry carries an ECDSA P-256 signature from the player's own browser key, the board bytes are SHA-256 stamped right now, and the compute cell is hash-verified before execution — no server, no trust, no screenshots.
powered_by: ECDSA P-256 WebCrypto signing per-entry (the-unbreakable-promise) + crypto.subtle SHA-256 freshness proof of ranking bytes (proof-of-freshness) + runVerifiedCell live leaderboard fetch and render (living-leaderboard)
weld: Transplant the-unbreakable-promise's ECDSA sign/verify loop to sign each player's tournament entry, proof-of-freshness's sha256Hex + arrayBuffer fetch to stamp the live ranking payload, and living-leaderboard's runVerifiedCell + parseTopRows to fetch and render the board — the signed player slot is injected into the freshness-stamped live board so every rank is either signed by its maker or provably current.
agent_scenario: When you need: a tamper-evident live ranking where every entry is cryptographically signed by the player's own browser key and the entire board is freshness-stamped so no stale screenshot can pass as current.
author: kody-w + Copilot
